metadata.description: Functions for trend analysis, time series forecasting, and temporal pattern detection

aggregate_by_time.description: Aggregate data by time periods (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly)
data_requirements: Time series data with date column
aggregate_by_time.output_description: Function that aggregates data by time periods

required_params.name: date_column
required_params.description: Column containing the date to aggregate by

optional_params.name: time_period
optional_params.description: Time period for aggregation ('D' for daily, 'W' for weekly, 'M' for monthly, 'Q' for quarterly, 'Y' for yearly) (default: 'D')

optional_params.name: aggregation
optional_params.description: Aggregation method (mean, sum, count, min, max) or dict mapping columns to methods (default: 'mean')

optional_params.name: fill_missing
optional_params.description: Whether to fill in missing time periods with NaN (default: True)

optional_params.name: include_current_period
optional_params.description: Whether to include the current (potentially incomplete) time period (default: False)

optional_params.name: time_name
optional_params.description: Name to use for the time column in the result (default: 'time_period')

optional_params.name: datetime_format
optional_params.description: Format string for parsing dates if they are not already datetime objects (optional)

outputs.description: Function that aggregates data by time periods

aggregate_by_time.instructions: This function must be called first in the pipeline to create time-aggregated data. All other trend analysis functions depend on this aggregation step. The date_column must contain valid datetime data, and metric_columns should contain numerical values to aggregate.

calculate_growth_rates.description: Calculate growth rates for aggregated metrics

optional_params.name: data
optional_params.description: DataFrame to calculate growth rates for (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: columns
optional_params.description: List of column names to calculate growth rates for (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: time_period
optional_params.description: Time period for annualization ('D', 'W', 'M', 'Q', 'Y') (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: window
optional_params.description: Window size for growth rate calculation (if None, compare to previous period) (optional)

optional_params.name: annualize
optional_params.description: Whether to annualize growth rates (useful for comparing different time periods) (default: False)

optional_params.name: method
optional_params.description: Method for calculating growth ('percentage', 'log', or 'cagr') (default: 'percentage')

calculate_growth_rates.instructions: This function requires aggregate_by_time() to be called first. It calculates growth rates for all metrics in the aggregated data. The window parameter determines how many periods to compare (1 = period-over-period, 4 = quarter-over-quarter for monthly data). Use annualize=True when comparing different time periods.

calculate_moving_average.description: Calculate moving averages for aggregated metrics

optional_params.name: data
optional_params.description: DataFrame to calculate moving averages for (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: columns
optional_params.description: List of column names to calculate moving averages for (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: window
optional_params.description: Window size for moving average (default: 7)

optional_params.name: method
optional_params.description: Method for calculating moving average ('simple', 'weighted', or 'exponential') (default: 'simple')

optional_params.name: center
optional_params.description: Whether to center the window (for simple and weighted) (default: False)

calculate_moving_average.instructions: This function requires aggregate_by_time() to be called first. It calculates moving averages for all metrics in the aggregated data. Simple MA gives equal weight to all values, weighted MA gives more weight to recent values, and exponential MA uses exponential weighting. Use center=True for symmetric windows.

decompose_trend.description: Decompose time series into trend, seasonal, and residual components

optional_params.name: data
optional_params.description: DataFrame to decompose (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: metric_column
optional_params.description: Column to decompose (required for pipeline usage, optional for direct usage)

optional_params.name: time_period
optional_params.description: Time period for extrapolation ('D', 'W', 'M', 'Q', 'Y') (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: model
optional_params.description: Type of seasonal component ('additive' or 'multiplicative') (default: 'additive')

optional_params.name: period
optional_params.description: Number of periods in a seasonal cycle (if None, will be estimated) (optional)

optional_params.name: extrapolate_trend
optional_params.description: Number of periods to extrapolate the trend into the future (optional)

decompose_trend.instructions: This function requires aggregate_by_time() to be called first. It decomposes a single metric into trend, seasonal, and residual components. Use additive model when seasonal variations are constant, multiplicative when they scale with the trend. The period parameter should match your data's seasonality (e.g., 12 for monthly data with yearly seasonality).

forecast_metric.description: Forecast future values of a metric

optional_params.name: data
optional_params.description: DataFrame to forecast from (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: metric_column
optional_params.description: Column to forecast (required for pipeline usage, optional for direct usage)

optional_params.name: time_period
optional_params.description: Time period for date generation ('D', 'W', 'M', 'Q', 'Y') (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: fperiods
optional_params.description: Number of periods to forecast (default: 12)

optional_params.name: fmethod
optional_params.description: Forecasting method ('holt_winters', 'linear', 'exponential') (default: 'holt_winters')

optional_params.name: seasonal_periods
optional_params.description: Number of periods in a seasonal cycle (if None, will be estimated) (optional)

optional_params.name: confidence_interval
optional_params.description: Confidence level for prediction intervals (default: 0.95)

forecast_metric.instructions: This function requires aggregate_by_time() to be called first. It forecasts future values for a single metric. Holt-Winters is best for data with trend and seasonality, linear for simple trends, and exponential for growth patterns. The confidence_interval determines the width of prediction bands.

calculate_statistical_trend.output_description: Function that tests for statistical trends

optional_params.name: data
optional_params.description: DataFrame to test for trends (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: metric_column
optional_params.description: Column to test for trend (required for pipeline usage, optional for direct usage)

optional_params.name: test_method
optional_params.description: Statistical test method ('mann_kendall', 't_test', 'spearman') (default: 'mann_kendall')

optional_params.name: alpha
optional_params.description: Significance level (default: 0.05)

optional_params.name: window
optional_params.description: Window size for rolling trend analysis (if None, test the entire series) (optional)

outputs.description: Function that tests for statistical trends

calculate_statistical_trend.instructions: This function requires aggregate_by_time() to be called first. It tests for statistically significant trends in a single metric. Mann-Kendall is robust to outliers, t-test assumes normality, and Spearman tests for monotonic trends. Use window parameter for rolling trend analysis. Lower alpha values require stronger evidence for significance.

compare_periods.description: Compare metrics between different time periods (e.g., year-over-year, month-over-month)
compare_periods.output_description: Function that compares metrics between periods

optional_params.name: data
optional_params.description: DataFrame to compare periods for (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: metric_column
optional_params.description: Column to compare (required for pipeline usage, optional for direct usage)

optional_params.name: time_period
optional_params.description: Time period for date calculations ('D', 'W', 'M', 'Q', 'Y') (for direct usage) (optional)

optional_params.name: comparison_type
optional_params.description: Type of comparison ('year_over_year', 'month_over_month', 'week_over_week', 'period_over_period') (default: 'year_over_year')

optional_params.name: n_periods
optional_params.description: Number of periods to go back for comparison (default: 1)

optional_params.name: aggregation
optional_params.description: Method to aggregate values within periods ('mean', 'sum', 'median', 'min', 'max') (default: 'mean')

outputs.description: Function that compares metrics between periods

compare_periods.instructions: This function requires aggregate_by_time() to be called first. It compares a single metric across different time periods. Year-over-year compares same periods in different years, month-over-month compares consecutive months, etc. The n_periods parameter determines how many periods back to compare.

get_top_metrics.description: Get top metrics based on growth, trend, or other criteria
data_requirements: Time series data with multiple metrics

optional_params.name: n
optional_params.description: Number of top metrics to return (default: 5)

optional_params.name: metric_type
optional_params.description: Type of metric to rank by ('growth', 'trend', 'volatility', 'absolute_change') (default: 'growth')

optional_params.name: ascending
optional_params.description: Whether to sort in ascending order (False = highest values first) (default: False)

get_top_metrics.instructions: This function requires other analysis functions to be called first depending on the metric_type. For 'growth', call calculate_growth_rates() first. For 'trend', call calculate_statistical_trend() first. For 'volatility' and 'absolute_change', call compare_periods() first. It ranks metrics based on the specified criteria and returns the top N performers.
